scenarios.title: Verify a partner by MPN id
gotchas: Omit mpnId to return the signed-in partner's profile.

scenarios.title: Get a list of customers
gotchas: Paged collection: response has totalCount, items[], and links (follow links.next for more pages).
Each item carries only companyProfile and relationshipToPartner; call get-customer-by-id for full detail.
Filter with &filter={url-encoded-json} to search by company name, domain, or reseller.
Supports both app+user and app-only.

scenarios.title: Create a customer

headers.name: MS-RequestId
headers.note: GUID for idempotency; reuse the same value on retries so the customer isn't created twice

requestFields.name: CompanyProfile.organizationRegistrationNumber
requestFields.note: required in some countries (RU, TR, BR, IN, UAE, ...)

requestFields.name: enableGDAPByDefault
requestFields.note: only honored in clouds that support GDAP

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch("https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers", { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json", "MS-RequestId": crypto.randomUUID(), "MS-CorrelationId": crypto.randomUUID() }, body });

gotchas: Returns 201 with a Customer that includes userCredentials (admin username/password) - save it, it cannot be retrieved later.
Idempotent: reuse the same MS-RequestId on retries to avoid creating duplicate customers.
Required body: BillingProfile (email, culture, language, companyName, defaultAddress) and CompanyProfile (domain).
A valid physical address is required; in the sandbox an invalid address makes the customer tenant undeletable.
organizationRegistrationNumber is mandatory for customers in certain countries/regions (e.g. Russia, Turkey, Brazil, India, UAE).
Do not pass a userCredentials password in the request; it is not accepted.
Also supports app-only.

scenarios.title: Get a customer by ID
gotchas: customer-id is the customer's Microsoft (Entra) tenant GUID.
Without DAP/GDAP access the response omits CompanyProfileAddress, CompanyProfileEmail, and CustomDomain.
Supports both app+user and app-only.

scenarios.title: Delete a customer from the integration sandbox

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}`, { method: "DELETE", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } });

gotchas: Sandbox (TIP) only - this breaks the partner-customer relationship and purges ALL resources for that tenant.
Returns 204 No Content on success.
All Azure reservations and software purchase orders must be canceled first or the delete fails.
A customer created with an invalid sandbox address cannot be deleted.

scenarios.title: Create a user account for a customer

requestFields.name: userPrincipalName
requestFields.note: must use the customer's own (non-federated) domain

requestFields.name: passwordProfile.password
requestFields.note: auto-generated if omitted; must meet complexity rules

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/users`, { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: App+User only - app-only is NOT supported. Requires a GDAP role (User Administrator or Directory Writers).
Returns 200 OK (not 201) with the created CustomerUser including its generated id.
userPrincipalName must use the customer's own (non-federated) domain.
passwordProfile.password must meet complexity rules; set forceChangePassword=true to require a reset on first sign-in.

scenarios.title: Get all user accounts for a customer
gotchas: App+User only - app-only is NOT supported. Requires a GDAP role (Directory Reader, Global Reader, or User Administrator).
For a single user, use /v1/customers/{customer-id}/users/{user-id}.

scenarios.title: Assign licenses to a user

requestFields.name: licensesToAssign
requestFields.note: each item has skuId and optional excludedPlans

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/users/${userId}/licenseupdates`, { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Returns 201 with the LicenseUpdate resource on success.
Fails if the license is not available in the customer tenant; get valid skuId values from get-subscribed-skus first.
licensesToAssign and licensesToRemove must reference SKUs from the same license group; verify the result with get-user-licenses.

scenarios.title: Get a list of products

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch("https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/products?country=US&targetView=OnlineServices", { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, Accept: "application/json" } });

gotchas: Both country and targetView are required.
targetView is an enum (Azure, AzureReservations, MicrosoftAzure, OnlineServices, Software, MarketplaceSaaS); use OnlineServices for new commerce (NCE) license-based products.
For Azure Reservations add reservationScope=AzurePlan.
403/400036 if the targetView is not allowed; 403/400030 if the targetSegment is not allowed.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Get a list of availabilities for a SKU

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/products/${productId}/skus/${skuId}/availabilities?country=US`, { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, Accept: "application/json" } });

gotchas: country is required; all segments except nonprofit are returned by default (pass targetSegment=nonprofit explicitly for those).
The availability's CatalogItemId is mutable and reissued regularly - always fetch a fresh availability right before adding a cart line item or placing an order.
For Azure Reservation SKUs add reservationScope=AzurePlan; add IncludeLifeCycleState=true for NCE license-based lifecycle state.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Create a cart

requestFields.name: lineItems[].termDuration
requestFields.note: ISO 8601 (P1M, P1Y); omit for Azure plan & perpetual software

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/carts`, { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Carts expire 7 days after creation. Success returns 201 with the populated Cart.
lineItems is required (>=1 CartLineItem). Line items are processed in array order - place any promotion-eligible (New-To-Offer) item FIRST.
billingCycle uses snake_case JSON enum values (one_time, monthly, none).
Azure plan and perpetual software line items do NOT support termDuration - omit it for those.
Set AttestationAccepted per line item when the offer enforces attestation.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Check out a cart

examples.csharp: var res = await http.PostAsync($"/v1/customers/{customerId}/carts/{cartId}/checkout", new StringContent("", Encoding.UTF8, "application/json")); // 201 Created
examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/carts/${cartId}/checkout`, { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" } });

gotchas: Empty request body (Content-Length: 0). Success returns 201 with a CartCheckoutResult containing an orders array.
Idempotent: re-checking out an already-checked-out cart returns the original result.
Provisioning is asynchronous - subscriptionId values may be absent in the response and appear later via Get Order.
Checkout fails on an expired cart (7-day limit).
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Get an order by ID
gotchas: Use this (or list-customer-orders) to retrieve subscriptionId values not yet provisioned at checkout time.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Get all of a customer's orders
gotchas: There is a delay of up to 15 minutes between order submission and appearance in this collection - don't poll immediately after checkout.
Optional ?billingType={monthly|annual|one_time|none} filters by billing cycle.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: List a customer's subscriptions
gotchas: Supports both app-only and app+user.
customer-tenant-id is the customer's Microsoft (Entra) tenant GUID.

scenarios.title: Get a subscription by ID
gotchas: Add-on subscriptions include parentSubscriptionId.
NCE subscriptions return extra fields (productType, termDuration, billingCycle, refundOptions, cancellationAllowedUntilDate).
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Update a subscription (quantity, status, autorenew)

requestFields.name: (full Subscription resource)
requestFields.note: GET the subscription, mutate, then PATCH the whole object back

requestFields.name: autoRenewEnabled
requestFields.note: include it or it silently resets to false

examples.csharp:
var req = new HttpRequestMessage(HttpMethod.Patch, $"/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}") { Content = content };
var res = await http.SendAsync(req);
examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/subscriptions/${subscriptionId}`, { method: "PATCH", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Send the FULL Subscription resource, not a partial patch: GET it first, mutate it, then PATCH it back.
If autoRenewEnabled or next-term instructions are set, include them in the body or they are silently reset.
Change quantity via Quantity; suspend/reactivate via Status (active/suspended); toggle renewal via AutoRenewEnabled.
Cancel an NCE subscription within its cancellation window by PATCHing Status to 'deleted' (see cancellationAllowedUntilDate).
Schedule changes for the next term (term/quantity/billing at renewal) via scheduledNextTermInstructions.
NCE license quantity can only be reduced within 7 days of purchase/renewal (else error 800090).
Billing cycle CANNOT be changed here (error 2054) - use update-order instead.
For SKU/term upgrades or trial-to-paid, use create-subscription-transition instead.
Long-running updates return 202 with a Location header to poll.

scenarios.title: Get a list of subscriptions by order
gotchas: Note the underscore: order_id, not orderId.
Returns a collection even when the order produced a single subscription.
Up to 15-minute delay after order submission before subscriptions appear.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Migrate a legacy subscription to New Commerce (NCE)

requestFields.name: termDuration
requestFields.note: ISO 8601; defaults to the legacy value

requestFields.name: addOnMigrations
requestFields.note: FLAT list of add-ons, not nested

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/migrations/newcommerce`, { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify({ currentSubscriptionId }) });

gotchas: Only currentSubscriptionId is required; termDuration, billingCycle, purchaseFullTerm, quantity, customTermEndDate are optional and let you change terms during migration.
Omitted properties carry forward the legacy subscription's values.
Base subscriptions migrate together with all active add-ons as a bundle; if any add-on is ineligible the whole bundle fails.
addOnMigrations must be a FLAT list, not nested.
Validate eligibility first with the validate-a-new-commerce-migration API.
To schedule rather than run immediately, POST to .../migrations/newcommerce/schedules.

scenarios.title: Get a collection of invoices
gotchas: size/offset are only honored for legacy invoices; for modern commercial purchases they are ignored.
Use the filter param (LeftFilter/RightFilter/Operator JSON, bounded by InvoiceDate) to avoid timeouts on large invoice sets.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Get an invoice by ID
gotchas: invoice-id is a string identifier, not a GUID.
App+User only (no app-only).
For modern commercial purchases this applies only to daily-rated usage and recons generated before 2025-05-15.

scenarios.title: Get a customer's Azure utilization records

examples.csharp:
var url = $"/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/utilizations/azure?start_time={Uri.EscapeDataString(start)}&end_time={Uri.EscapeDataString(end)}&granularity=daily&show_details=true";
var res = await http.GetAsync(url);
examples.typescript:
const qs = new URLSearchParams({ start_time: start, end_time: end, granularity: "daily", show_details: "true" });
const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/subscriptions/${subscriptionId}/utilizations/azure?${qs}`, { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } });

gotchas: start_time and end_time are REQUIRED (UTC date-time offset). granularity (daily default), show_details (default true), and size (max 1000) are optional.
NOT supported for Azure plans (only legacy MS-AZR-0145P); for Azure plans use the invoice consumption line-item APIs.
If data isn't ready in billing yet, returns 204 with a Retry-After header.
Paged - follow the next link.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Get the partner's current account balance

examples.csharp: var res = await http.GetAsync("/v1/invoices/summary"); // read BalanceAmount

gotchas: The balance is exposed via InvoiceSummary.BalanceAmount; there is no dedicated balance endpoint.
Use /v1/invoices/summaries (plural) for one summary per currency.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Validate an address

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch("https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/validations/address", { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Prevalidate addresses before create-customer or profile updates; the caller picks the final address from the result.
Country must be the two-character ISO alpha-2 code; required fields vary by country/region.
Only Verified or VerifiedShippable are safe to use directly; other statuses need user confirmation.

scenarios.title: Verify domain availability

examples.typescript:
const res = await fetch("https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/domains/contoso.onmicrosoft.com", { method: "HEAD", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } });
const available = res.status === 404;

gotchas: Use the HEAD method - there is no response body; rely on the status code.
404 Not Found means the domain IS available; 200 OK means it is already in use.
The {domain} segment is the full domain string, e.g. contoso.onmicrosoft.com.

scenarios.title: Get a record of Partner Center activity
gotchas: Activity log data is limited to the previous 90 days; a startDate older than 90 days returns 400.
Without startDate, results default to the previous 30 days. Dates use yyyy-mm-dd.
filter must be url-encoded JSON with Field/Value/Operator (fields: CompanyName substring, CustomerId equals, ResourceType equals); matching is case-insensitive.
Results are paged.

scenarios.title: Get all service requests for a customer
gotchas: App+User only (no app-only).
For a single service request use GET /v1/servicerequests/{servicerequest-id}; that endpoint accepts an IncludeOrganizationRequests header for the Support Request Admin role.

scenarios.title: Get per-request MFA compliance for App+User API calls
gotchas: App+User only. Returns the most recent App+User API requests, capped at 10K items.
Each item exposes mfaCompliant (true/false) plus upn, objectId, applicationId, operationName - use it to find non-MFA-compliant callers before the MFA enforcement date.
This is request-level telemetry, not a static per-user enrollment record.

scenarios.title: Get customer licenses usage information
gotchas: App+User only. Returns CustomerLicensesUsageInsights (workloadName, productName, licensesActive, licensesQualified, usagePercent).
Superseded by the workload-level GET /v1/analytics/commercial/usage/license/, but this per-customer endpoint remains documented and valid.

scenarios.title: Create a New Commerce transfer (billing ownership)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com; caller must be an Admin agent

requestFields.name: targetProductTypes
requestFields.note: AzureSubscription, AzureReservation, AzureSavingsPlan, OnlineServicesNCE; null = all

requestFields.name: partnerOnRecord
requestFields.note: MPN ID; only when target is an indirect provider

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/transfers`, { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: The TARGET (new) partner creates the transfer; the SOURCE partner later submits it. Only Admin agents can create transfers.
transferType must be 3 (NewCommerce) - this endpoint is not for legacy subscriptions.
Customer must already have a reseller relationship with the target partner.
Returns 201 with status Pending. Common errors: 900400, 900160, 20002.

scenarios.title: Get a transfer by ID
gotchas: Either the source or target partner can read transfer status (Pending -> InProgress -> Complete).
Target partners cannot see line items until the transfer completes (new subscription/order IDs are issued then).

scenarios.title: Get a customer's Azure reservations (entitlements)
gotchas: Partner Center has no 'reservation orders' endpoint; reservations are 'reservedinstance' entitlements, each carrying a referenceOrder.id and entitledArtifacts links.
Omit entitlementType to return all entitlement types; add showExpiry=true for expiry dates.
Reservation orders have billingCycle one_time; fetch the order itself via get-order-by-id with referenceOrder.id.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Get customer agreements (MCA confirmation)
gotchas: App+User only (no app-only).
If agreementType is omitted it defaults to MicrosoftCloudAgreement; pass MicrosoftCustomerAgreement explicitly for MCA confirmations.
Agreement resource is supported only in the Microsoft public cloud (not 21Vianet or US Gov).
Returns a paged collection.

scenarios.title: Get the Azure rate card (real-time prices)

headers.name: X-Locale
headers.note: e.g. en-US; also drives region/currency defaults

gotchas: currency (ISO, default USD) and region (ISO country, default US) are optional; prices vary by market.
Response can be very large (~1.5 MB) - stream/parse efficiently.
All rates are pretax (isTaxIncluded is always false).
Use /v1/ratecards/azure-shared for Azure Partner Shared Services pricing.

scenarios.title: List GDAP delegated admin relationships (Microsoft Graph)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com (NOT partnercenter); scope DelegatedAdminRelationship.Read.All

gotchas: This is a Microsoft Graph API, NOT Partner Center: host graph.microsoft.com, path starts with /v1.0/. A Partner Center token will not work.
Needs a Graph token with scope DelegatedAdminRelationship.Read.All (or ReadWrite.All); caller is in the partner tenant.
Supports OData $select/$filter/$top (max 300)/$orderby; paged via @odata.nextLink.
Not available in China (21Vianet); available in Global and US Gov.

scenarios.title: Get eligible subscription transitions (NCE)
gotchas: eligibilityType is optional (default immediate); use the returned catalogItemId as toCatalogItemId in create-subscription-transition.
Distinct from GET .../transitions, which returns transition history rather than eligible transitions.
Empty list for trials or when eligibilityType=scheduled (you can only transition into an existing subscription via an immediate transition).

scenarios.title: Create a subscription transition (NCE upgrade / trial-to-paid)

requestFields.name: toSubscriptionId
requestFields.note: transition into an existing subscription (immediate only)

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/subscriptions/${subscriptionId}/transitions`, { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Two-step: GET transitionEligibilities first to get a valid toCatalogItemId, then POST.
Used to upgrade an NCE subscription to a target SKU/term and to convert an NCE trial to paid.
transition_with_license_transfer needs Directory Writer (GDAP); provisioning is asynchronous (response has an Events array).

scenarios.title: Upgrade a customer to the Azure plan

requestFields.name: productFamily
requestFields.note: "Azure" (upgrades legacy MS-AZR-0145P to the Azure plan)

examples.csharp: var res = await http.PostAsync("/v1/productupgrades", content); var location = res.Headers.Location; // productUpgrades/{id}
examples.typescript: const res = await fetch("https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/productupgrades", { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Global /v1/productupgrades collection (not subscription-scoped); the customer is identified in the body.
Returns 202 Accepted with an empty body and a Location header (productUpgrades/{id}) to poll; app+user only.
Check eligibility first via POST /v1/productUpgrades/eligibility. After upgrade, Get Subscriptions returns only the Azure plan.

scenarios.title: Get invoice billed reconciliation line items

headers.name: MS-ContinuationToken
headers.note: from the previous response's links.next when seekOperation=Next

gotchas: billing-provider: OneTime (modern), or Azure/Office (legacy). invoice-line-item-type: BillingLineItems or UsageLineItems.
OneTime pages via seekOperation=Next + the MS-ContinuationToken header (from links.next); Azure/Office use size+offset. Default/max size 2000.
This v1 API is in feature freeze; for new-commerce billed recon after 2025-04-21 use the async Graph v2 API.

scenarios.title: Get unbilled reconciliation line items

headers.name: MS-ContinuationToken
headers.note: from the previous response's links.next when seekOperation=Next

gotchas: Literal path /invoices/unbilled/lineitems (no invoice-id). Required query: provider, invoicelineitemtype, currencycode, period (current|previous).
Pages via size (max 2000) + seekOperation=Next with MS-ContinuationToken; allow up to 24h for unbilled data.
This v1 NCE API is deprecated effective 2026-03-15; use the async v2 GA unbilled API.

scenarios.title: Download an invoice statement (PDF)

examples.typescript:
const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/invoices/${invoiceId}/documents/statement`, { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } });
const pdf = Buffer.from(await res.arrayBuffer());

gotchas: App+User only. Response is a binary PDF (Content-Type application/pdf) - read it as bytes, not JSON.
Path is /documents/statement (singular). For Taiwan tax receipts use .../receipts/{receipt-id}/documents/statement.

scenarios.title: Get available offer promotions
gotchas: country and segment are required; segment currently supports only 'commercial'.
Returns only currently-active promotions (by start/end dates). Covers NCE license-based promotions.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Get a promotion by ID
gotchas: country is required; promotion-id is a delimited product:sku:availability string - URL-encode it.
Returns the promotion ignoring start/end dates (works after expiry - useful for reconciliation).
Response may include promotionConstraints for eligibility checks. Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Get a product by ID
gotchas: country is required; 404 with error 400013 if not found.
A product is a grouping construct and is not itself purchasable - drill into SKUs/availabilities.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Get a SKU by ID
gotchas: country is required; 404 with error 400013 (product) or 400018 (SKU) if not found.
If purchasePrerequisites contains 'InventoryCheck' you must call Check Inventory before ordering.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Confirm customer acceptance of the MCA (create agreement)

requestFields.name: primaryContact
requestFields.note: who accepted: firstName, lastName, email (phoneNumber optional)

requestFields.name: templateId
requestFields.note: from Get agreement metadata for the MCA

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/agreements`, { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: App+User only; public cloud only (not 21Vianet or US Gov). Get the templateId via Get agreement metadata first.
201 Created on success; userId is derived from the App+User token.
Re-creating with identical contact fields returns error 600061 - vary at least one field to create another.

scenarios.title: Get a download link for the MCA template
gotchas: App+User only; public cloud + MCA only. Top-level /v1/agreementtemplates path (not under /customers).
country/language default to US/en-US; values are case-sensitive.
downloadUri (Word) and displayUri (preview) are freshly generated and EXPIRE after five minutes.

scenarios.title: Get a customer's qualifications
gotchas: Supports both app-only and app+user (unlike update-customer-qualification, which is app+user only).
Returns an array; a customer can hold multiple (e.g. Education + StateOwnedEntity).
Values: Education, GovernmentCommunityCloud, StateOwnedEntity, Nonprofit, None; vetted types carry vettingStatus.

scenarios.title: Set a customer's qualification

requestFields.name: qualification
requestFields.note: settable: Education, GovernmentCommunityCloud, StateOwnedEntity (None/Nonprofit cannot be set)

requestFields.name: educationSegment
requestFields.note: required when Education: K12 or HigherEducation

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/qualifications`, { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: POST (not PUT); app+user only. Only Education, GovernmentCommunityCloud, StateOwnedEntity can be set.
Education requires educationSegment (K12 or HigherEducation); GCC requires a validationCode + a GDAP role.
Vetted types (Education/GCC) start in a review/vetting flow rather than immediately Approved.

scenarios.title: Check inventory for catalog items before ordering

requestFields.name: InventoryContext
requestFields.note: per-SKU keys from the SKU's inventoryVariables (e.g. customerId, azureSubscriptionId, armRegionName)

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch("https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/extensions/product/checkInventory?country=US", { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Only needed for SKUs whose purchasePrerequisites contains 'InventoryCheck'; skip otherwise.
Required InventoryContext keys vary by SKU (declared in the SKU's inventoryVariables).
Items not found in the catalog are silently omitted from the result.

scenarios.title: Update an order (legacy billing cycle / cancel)

requestFields.name: Status
requestFields.note: set to 'cancelled' to cancel the order (sandbox)

examples.csharp:
var req = new HttpRequestMessage(HttpMethod.Patch, $"/v1/customers/{customerId}/orders/{orderId}") { Content = content };
var res = await http.SendAsync(req);
examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/orders/${orderId}`, { method: "PATCH", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Billing-cycle change here is for LEGACY subscriptions only; for NCE use update-subscription instead.
Out of scope: trials, monthly/Azure offers, inactive or online-services license-based subscriptions.
To cancel an order, PATCH the same path with status 'cancelled' (documented for the sandbox).

scenarios.title: Get a service request by ID

headers.name: IncludeOrganizationRequests
headers.note: authorizes the Support Request Admin role to read an org-owned request

gotchas: App+User only. There is no REST endpoint to list support topics or to create a service request (portal-only).
Pass IncludeOrganizationRequests to read a request owned by the organization.

scenarios.title: Update a service request (add note / close)

requestFields.name: Status
requestFields.note: none | open | closed | attention_needed (set closed to close)

examples.csharp:
var req = new HttpRequestMessage(HttpMethod.Patch, $"/v1/servicerequests/{serviceRequestId}") { Content = content };
var res = await http.SendAsync(req);
examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/servicerequests/${serviceRequestId}`, { method: "PATCH", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: App+User only. Send only the fields you want to change.
Add notes via the singular NewNote object (the Notes array is read-only).

scenarios.title: Upload devices as a new batch (Autopilot)

requestFields.name: devices
requestFields.note: each device needs an accepted id combo (hardwareHash, productKey, serialNumber+oemManufacturerName+modelName, ...)

examples.csharp: var res = await http.PostAsync($"/v1/customers/{customerId}/deviceBatches", content); var location = res.Headers.Location;
examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/deviceBatches`, { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Returns 202 Accepted with a Location header (/customers/{id}/batchJobStatus/{tracking-id}) - poll it for status.
App+User only. Each device must satisfy one of the accepted id field combinations.
Add devices to an EXISTING batch via update-devices instead of reusing a batchId.

scenarios.title: Get a customer's device batches
gotchas: Each batch item has a devicesLink to fetch that batch's devices.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Add devices to an existing batch

requestFields.name: (array)
requestFields.note: bare JSON array of Device objects (NOT wrapped), each with an accepted id combo

examples.csharp: var res = await http.PostAsync($"/v1/customers/{customerId}/deviceBatches/{batchId}/devices", content); var location = res.Headers.Location;
examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/deviceBatches/${batchId}/devices`, { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Body is a bare JSON ARRAY of Device objects (unlike create-device-batch, which wraps them).
Returns 202 Accepted with a Location header to poll. Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Get a customer's configuration policies (Autopilot)
gotchas: The wire path is /policies (the SDK surface is named ConfigurationPolicies).
policySettings is an enum: remove_oem_preinstalls, oobe_user_not_local_admin, skip_express_settings, skip_oem_registration, skip_eula.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

scenarios.title: Create a GDAP relationship (Microsoft Graph)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope DelegatedAdminRelationship.ReadWrite.All

requestFields.name: displayName
requestFields.note: unique across the partner's relationships, max 50 chars

requestFields.name: accessDetails.unifiedRoles
requestFields.note: each has roleDefinitionId (Entra role template GUID)

requestFields.name: customer.tenantId
requestFields.note: omit to let any customer approve; set to target a specific customer

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch("https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/tenantRelationships/delegatedAdminRelationships", { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${graphToken}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Graph API (graph.microsoft.com), not Partner Center. Returns 201 with a Location header.
Creating it is step 1: then POST a relationshipRequest (lockForApproval), have the customer approve, then create accessAssignments.
Not available in China (21Vianet).

scenarios.title: Get a GDAP relationship by ID (Microsoft Graph)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope DelegatedAdminRelationship.Read.All

gotchas: Graph API. The id is a compound {partnerTenantGuid}-{customerTenantGuid}.
Response carries an @odata.etag. Not available in China (21Vianet).

scenarios.title: Get a reseller relationship request URL to send a customer

examples.csharp: var res = await http.GetAsync("/v1/customers/relationshiprequests"); // body.url is the invitation link

gotchas: A GET that returns a personalized { url } invitation link to send the customer; no request body.
App+User only. The customer accepts it in the Microsoft 365 admin center.
Not available for customer tenants with B2C/CIAM features enabled.

scenarios.title: Get Azure fraud events

headers.name: X-NewEventsModel
headers.note: set true for the new model (EventType, PageSize, PageNumber + richer alert types)

gotchas: All query params optional. Base model: EventStatus, SubscriptionId.
With X-NewEventsModel: true you also get EventType, PageSize, PageNumber (both must be nonzero to page).
Microsoft is steering partners to the Graph partner security alerts API; this remains usable during transition.

scenarios.title: Get co-sell referrals / leads (Partner API)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://api.partner.microsoft.com; user needs Referral Admin/User role

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch("https://api.partner.microsoft.com/v1.0/engagements/referrals?$orderby=createdDateTime desc", { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } });

gotchas: Host is api.partner.microsoft.com (the Partner API), NOT api.partnercenter.microsoft.com. App+User only.
Supports OData $select/$filter/$orderby/$top; page via @odata.nextLink.
Unresponded incoming leads are archived as Expired after 14 days.

scenarios.title: Create a referral / co-sell deal (Partner API)

requestFields.name: type
requestFields.note: Independent or Shared (indicative; actual type derived from consent)

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch("https://api.partner.microsoft.com/v1.0/engagements/referrals", { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Host is api.partner.microsoft.com. App+User only.
Co-sell (Shared) is a two-step flow: POST the partner-led deal, then POST a Microsoft referral linked via the returned engagementId.
For Services co-sell, PartnerRole/SolutionArea/SolutionPlay are required under details.requirements.additionalRequirements.

scenarios.title: Get the Azure Partner Shared Services rate card
gotchas: The 'shared' variant for Azure Partner Shared Services; the standard one is /v1/ratecards/azure.
currency/region optional (default from the partner profile). All rates are pretax.

scenarios.title: Get partner-level Azure usage analytics
gotchas: Note the /partner/v1/ path prefix (not /v1/). App+User only; CSP partners only.
OData params: top (max 10000), skip, filter, orderby, groupby, aggregationLevel (day|week|month).
Response includes a next link when more rows exist than 'top'.

scenarios.title: Assign a directory role to a customer user

requestFields.name: Id
requestFields.note: object id of the user to add to the role

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/directoryroles/${roleId}/usermembers`, { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: App+User only; requires the GDAP Privileged Role Administrator role.
role-id is the directory role's GUID (from the customer's directoryroles collection).
Returns 201 Created on success.

scenarios.title: Reset a customer user's password

examples.csharp:
var req = new HttpRequestMessage(HttpMethod.Patch, $"/v1/customers/{customerId}/users/{userId}/resetpassword") { Content = content };
var res = await http.SendAsync(req);
examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/users/${userId}/resetpassword`, { method: "PATCH", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Use the dedicated /resetpassword sub-resource - NOT the generic update-user PATCH.
App+User only; needs the GDAP User Administrator (or Privileged Authentication Administrator) role.
Not supported in US Gov - use the Azure portal there.

scenarios.title: Restore a deleted customer user

requestFields.name: State
requestFields.note: set to 'active' to restore; other fields auto-restore

examples.csharp:
var req = new HttpRequestMessage(HttpMethod.Patch, $"/v1/customers/{customerId}/users/{userId}") { Content = content };
var res = await http.SendAsync(req);
examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/users/${userId}`, { method: "PATCH", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Only within the 30-day window after deletion; after that the account is purged.
App+User only; needs GDAP User Administrator or Directory Writer.
Find the deleted user-id via the deleted (UserState=Inactive) users list.

scenarios.title: Get the partner billing profile
gotchas: App+User only. billingCurrency is fixed at account creation and cannot be changed.
Returns companyName, address, primaryContact, purchaseOrderNumber, taxId, billingCurrency, billingDay.

scenarios.title: Update the partner billing profile

examples.csharp:
var req = new HttpRequestMessage(HttpMethod.Put, "/v1/profiles/billing") { Content = content };
var res = await http.SendAsync(req);
examples.typescript: const res = await fetch("https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/profiles/billing", { method: "PUT", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: PUT (full replace), not PATCH: GET it, mutate, then PUT the whole resource back.
App+User only. Modern 'G' invoices may not reflect PO-number changes made here (may need a support request).

scenarios.title: Get the partner legal business profile
gotchas: Path segment is 'legalbusiness' (one word).
Returns companyName, address, primaryContact, companyApproverAddress/Email, vettingStatus/vettingSubStatus.

scenarios.title: Get the partner support profile
gotchas: App+User only. GET path is /v1/profiles/support but the UPDATE path is /v1/profiles/supportprofile - they differ.
Returns email, telephone, website.

scenarios.title: Get the partner organization profile
gotchas: App+User only. Update via PUT /v1/profiles/organization (full resource).
Returns id, companyName, defaultAddress, tenantId, domain, email, language, culture.

scenarios.title: Get Microsoft 365 service health overviews (Microsoft Graph)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope ServiceHealth.Read.All

gotchas: Microsoft Graph (not Partner Center): reads the signed-in/own tenant, or a customer tenant via GDAP/app context.
Add $expand=issues for active issues per service; paged at 100, follow @odata.nextLink.

scenarios.title: List Microsoft 365 service health issues (Microsoft Graph)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope ServiceHealth.Read.All

gotchas: Microsoft Graph (not Partner Center). Each issue carries classification, status, service, isResolved, posts.
Default page size 100; set Prefer: odata.maxpagesize and follow @odata.nextLink.

scenarios.title: List Message center posts (Microsoft Graph)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope ServiceMessage.Read.All

gotchas: Microsoft Graph (not Partner Center). Scope is ServiceMessage.Read.All (NOT ServiceHealth.Read.All).
Message center posts (IDs like MCxxxxxx); body content is HTML. Paged at 100, follow @odata.nextLink.

scenarios.title: Download an invoice receipt statement (Taiwan tax receipt)

examples.typescript:
const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/invoices/${invoiceId}/receipts/${receiptId}/documents/statement`, { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } });
const pdf = Buffer.from(await res.arrayBuffer());

gotchas: Taiwan tax receipts only - not a general invoice download. Response is a binary PDF (read as bytes).
App+User only. Needs both an invoice id and a receipt id (from the invoice's tax-receipt collection).

scenarios.title: Export billed reconciliation line items (Microsoft Graph, async v2)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope PartnerBilling.Read.All

examples.csharp: var res = await http.PostAsync("https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/reports/partners/billing/reconciliation/billed/export", content); var op = res.Headers.Location;
examples.typescript: const res = await fetch("https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/reports/partners/billing/reconciliation/billed/export", { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${graphToken}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Microsoft Graph v2; replaces the older v1 billed-recon endpoint. Returns 202 + a Location operation URL - poll it (honor Retry-After), then download the manifest's Azure Blob files.
CSP partners only; PartnerBilling.Read.All. Global cloud only (not US Gov / China).

scenarios.title: Export unbilled reconciliation line items (Microsoft Graph, async v2)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope PartnerBilling.Read.All

examples.csharp: var res = await http.PostAsync("https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/reports/partners/billing/reconciliation/unbilled/export", content); var op = res.Headers.Location;
examples.typescript: const res = await fetch("https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/reports/partners/billing/reconciliation/unbilled/export", { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${graphToken}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Microsoft Graph v2; both currencyCode and billingPeriod (current|last) are required. Returns 202 + Location - poll then download from the manifest.
CSP partners only; PartnerBilling.Read.All. Daily-rated usage takes ~24h to appear. Global cloud only.

scenarios.title: Create a GDAP access assignment (Microsoft Graph)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope DelegatedAdminRelationship.ReadWrite.All

requestFields.name: accessContainer.accessContainerId
requestFields.note: object id of the partner security group whose members get the roles

requestFields.name: accessDetails.unifiedRoles
requestFields.note: each has roleDefinitionId (Entra role template GUID); must be a subset of the relationship's roles

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/tenantRelationships/delegatedAdminRelationships/${relationshipId}/accessAssignments`, { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${graphToken}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: This is what actually grants admin-on-behalf-of (AOBO): it binds a partner security group to a set of Entra roles in the customer tenant. Do it only after the relationship is active (customer approved).
Returns 201 with a Location header; the new assignment starts status 'pending' and transitions to 'active'. unifiedRoles must be a subset of the relationship's roles.

App-only calls require provisioning the service principal appId 2832473f-ec63-45fb-976f-5d45a7d4bb91 (Partner Customer Delegated Administration) in the partner tenant. Not available in China (21Vianet).

scenarios.title: List GDAP access assignments (Microsoft Graph)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope DelegatedAdminRelationship.Read.All

gotchas: Each item carries an @odata.etag; capture it to send as If-Match when updating that assignment.
Graph API, not Partner Center. Not available in China (21Vianet).

scenarios.title: Update a GDAP access assignment's roles (Microsoft Graph)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope DelegatedAdminRelationship.ReadWrite.All

requestFields.name: accessDetails.unifiedRoles
requestFields.note: the new full set of roleDefinitionId values for the assignment (subset of the relationship's roles)

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/tenantRelationships/delegatedAdminRelationships/${relationshipId}/accessAssignments/${assignmentId}`, { method: "PATCH", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${graphToken}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: Send the complete desired unifiedRoles set, not a delta - it replaces the assignment's roles. Roles must remain a subset of the relationship's roles.
Returns 200; only roles can be changed (not the security group). Use list-gdap-access-assignments to get the assignment id and etag first. Not available in China (21Vianet).

scenarios.title: Lock/terminate a GDAP relationship via a request (Microsoft Graph)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope DelegatedAdminRelationship.ReadWrite.All

requestFields.name: action
requestFields.note: lockForApproval (finalize a 'created' relationship for customer approval), terminate (end an 'active' one), approve / reject (indirect reseller on an 'approvalPending' one)

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/tenantRelationships/delegatedAdminRelationships/${relationshipId}/requests`, { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${graphToken}`, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body });

gotchas: action lockForApproval is GDAP step 3: after it, send the customer the approval link https://admin.microsoft.com/AdminPortal/Home#/partners/invitation/granularAdminRelationships/{request-id} (the id from this response). On approval the relationship becomes active.
action terminate ends an active relationship; access assignments stop working. The action enum is evolvable - send Prefer: include-unknown-enum-members to receive 'reject'.
Returns 201 with a Location header to the created request. Not available in China (21Vianet).

scenarios.title: List delegated admin customers (Microsoft Graph)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope DelegatedAdminRelationship.Read.All

gotchas: Returns the customers the partner has any GDAP relationship with - a tenant-wide view independent of a single relationship id.
Each delegatedAdminCustomer exposes a displayName and tenantId; drill into serviceManagementDetails for the admin URLs. Not available in China (21Vianet).

scenarios.title: Delete a GDAP relationship (Microsoft Graph)

headers.name: Authorization
headers.note: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope DelegatedAdminRelationship.ReadWrite.All

examples.typescript: const res = await fetch(`https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/tenantRelationships/delegatedAdminRelationships/${relationshipId}`, { method: "DELETE", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${graphToken}` } });

gotchas: Only a relationship still in the 'created' state can be deleted; an 'active' relationship must be ended with create-gdap-relationship-request (action terminate) instead.
Returns 204 No Content. Not available in China (21Vianet).

scenarios.title: Get a list of Azure entitlements for a subscription
gotchas: For an Azure plan, pass the Azure plan's subscription (plan) id as {subscription-id}; the response lists the underlying Azure usage subscriptions (azureEntitlementId values) created under it.
Each azureEntitlementId is what you use to cancel a single Azure subscription (POST .../azureEntitlements/{entitlement-id}) without cancelling the whole plan.
Also supports standalone App (app-only) credentials.

scenarios.title: Register a subscription for Azure reservations

headers.name: MS-RequestId
headers.note: GUID for idempotency; reuse on retries so the registration runs once

examples.csharp:
var res = await http.PostAsync($"/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/registrations", null);
var statusUrl = res.Headers.Location;
examples.typescript:
const res = await fetch(`https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com/v1/customers/${customerId}/subscriptions/${subscriptionId}/registrations`, { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } });
const statusUrl = res.headers.get("Location");

gotchas: Registration is a prerequisite before purchasing Azure reservations (Reserved VM Instances) on a subscription.
No request body. Returns 202 Accepted with a Location header (.../registrationstatus) - poll it with get-subscription-registration-status until status is 'registered'.
For new commerce, register the Azure plan first: pass the Azure plan id as {subscription-id}.

scenarios.title: Get subscription registration status
gotchas: status is one of 'registered', 'registering', or 'notregistered'; poll after register-subscription until it reads 'registered'.
This is the URL returned in the Location header of register-subscription; you can call it directly with the same {subscription-id}.

scenarios.title: Get the signed-in partner's indirect resellers or providers
gotchas: relationship_type is required: use IsIndirectCloudSolutionProviderOf when signed in as an indirect provider (returns your resellers), or IsIndirectResellerOf when signed in as a reseller (returns your providers). See enum partnerRelationshipType.
App+User credentials only - this is a two-tier CSP (indirect) operation. Each item exposes the partner's mpnId, used as PartnerIdOnRecord when placing orders for a reseller's customer.
Only meaningful for indirect providers/resellers; direct-bill partners have no such relationships.

scenarios.title: Get the partners (indirect resellers/providers) of a customer
gotchas: Returns which partners have a reseller relationship with this customer; relationshipType reads e.g. is_indirect_cloud_solution_provider_of and each item carries the partner's mpnId.
App+User credentials only. Use this to confirm both you and your indirect provider hold a reseller relationship before transacting on the customer's behalf.
To establish a missing relationship, send the customer the invitation link from get-reseller-relationship-url.

scenarios.title: Get a customer's available licenses (subscribed SKUs)
gotchas: Step before assign-licenses: each SubscribedSku exposes productSku.id (the skuId you pass in licensesToAssign) plus availableUnits/consumedUnits so you can check seats before assigning.
App+User credentials only; needs a GDAP role such as Directory Reader, Global Reader, User Administrator, or License Administrator.
Returns the default license group's SKUs; a license can only be assigned to a user in the same group.

scenarios.title: Get the licenses assigned to a user
gotchas: Verifies what assign-licenses applied: each License lists its skuId and the assigned/excluded servicePlans.
App+User credentials only; needs a GDAP role such as Directory Reader, License Administrator, User Administrator, or Directory Writers.
Returns the default license group's assignments for the user.
